sn43.1:1.2: “Monks, I will teach you the unfabricated and the path leading to the unfabricated.
sn43.1:1.3: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.” “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to the Blessed One.
sn43.1:1.4: The Blessed One said, “Which, monks, is the unfabricated?
sn43.1:1.5: Whatever is the ending of passion, the ending of aversion, the ending of delusion:
sn43.1:1.7: “And which is the path leading to the unfabricated?
sn43.1:1.9: This is called the path leading to the unfabricated.
sn43.1:2.1: Thus, monks, I have taught you the unfabricated and the path leading to the unfabricated.
sn43.1:2.2: Whatever a sympathetic teacher should do—seeking the welfare of his disciples, out of sympathy for them—that have I done for you.
sn43.1:2.3: Over there are the roots of trees; over there, empty dwellings. Practice jhāna, monks. Don’t be heedless. Don’t later fall into remorse. This is our message to you all.”
